title: Calorophus vestitus
taxonomy: Calorophus vestitus is classified as a species of hummingbird in the family Trochilidae. It is specifically categorized as a member of the genus Calorophus, which is a group of birds found in Central America. Calorophus vestitus is further divided into the kingdom Animalia, phylum Chordata, and class Aves. Within the class Aves, it is placed in the order Apodiformes, followed by the family Trochilidae and genus Calorophus. This comprehensive classification reflects the unique characteristics and evolutionary history of the Calorophus vestitus.

intro: Calorophus vestitus, also known as Desert Marigold, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and pinnate leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Calorophus vestitus include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Desert Marigold is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and its extracts are also used in topical creams and ointments to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
